summary: Compact assistant/runtime guidance that separates system governance, editable project rules, and detailed startup docs.

file_roles.CONTRIBUTING.md: Authoritative human commit and branch protocol.
file_roles.AGENTS.md: Repo-wide system governance, routing, verification, and completion policy.
file_roles..verlyn/runtime_context.json: Compact runtime summary for assistants and compressed sessions.
file_roles.RULES.md: Editable project-specific guidance for people and assistants.
file_roles.Documentation/guides/VERLYN_ASSISTANT_ST: Detailed assistant startup order, API usage, and helper-path guidance.
file_roles.Documentation/guides/VERLYN_AGENT_WORKFL: Assistant session loop after startup is complete.

control_path_priority: Verlyn API routes when helpers do not expose the exact mutation
Raw verlyn CLI only when the helper does not wrap the capability yet
Direct edits to workflow files only when the product path is blocked or the change is explicitly repairing that path
workflow_defaults: Treat Verlyn-managed changes and tasks as the active workflow source of truth.
Use RULES.md for editable project guidance and keep AGENTS.md authoritative for system policy.

After session compaction, compressed-summary recovery, or other resume paths, reread AGENTS.md, RULES.md, .verlyn/runtime_context.json, Documentation/AI_USAGE_POLICY.md, and Documentation/guides/VERLYN_ASSISTANT_STARTUP.md before continuing.
Run python scripts/verlyn_workflow.py assistant-startup --json after resume so the governed startup contract is reloaded and the visible governance receipt is refreshed.
The first user-facing response after compaction or resume must explicitly say that governance was reloaded and required repo rules were reread; hidden helper JSON or receipt files are not enough.
Run python scripts/verlyn_workflow.py assert-edit-route before manual code edits; use a recorded active change route or an explicit direct-work exception instead of relying on memory.
Update Documentation/guides/VERLYN_ASSISTANT_STARTUP.md when workflow, API, auth/session, startup order, or helper behavior changes.
Prefer reusable components and shared helpers when the same UI or workflow pattern appears in multiple places.
